content: Language learning vocabulary acquisition of the term "reflection" (as covered in the high school English curriculum) can be used to bootstrap more stable, human-aligned LLM self-reflection mechanisms by grounding abstract agent self-critique in the shared lexical semantics humans use to practice deliberate reflection. Just as human learners internalize the concept of reflection through explicit vocabulary instruction, LLMs can be fine-tuned to anchor their self-reflection loops to this common human conceptual schema, reducing misalignment between how agents and humans interpret self-critique.
